goal: Prevent stale reviewer artifacts from satisfying JSC-363 review coverage when live PR head state has moved.

feedbackSignal.summary: A PR #327 review-thread audit subagent wrote artifacts that claimed head ab27286b6658440c49d7de8ee1aac7f3371c36d9 and unresolved review concerns while live GitHub evidence showed head 33e99c695c6cb6f67301948e1b9013deb5ed2037, green checks, and all review threads resolved.
feedbackSignal.source: live PR truth refresh plus subagent artifact comparison

rootOperationalFailure: Reviewer coverage validated artifact presence and WROTE markers but did not require reviewer artifacts to bind their claims to the current PR head SHA.

durableImprovement.behavior: When a reviewer manifest declares expectedHeadSha, required reviewer artifacts must contain the same head SHA before the artifact can count as completed. Missing or mismatched artifact head evidence fails closed.

nonGoals: Do not mutate CircleCI state or use CircleCI credentials unless a CircleCI check fails or logs are opaque.
Do not claim PR #324 supersession, PR #327 merge readiness, Linear closure, Judge/PM readiness, or goal completion from this validator slice alone.
Do not change public review workflow semantics for manifests that do not opt into expectedHeadSha.

validationPlan: pnpm exec biome check scripts/validate-reviewer-coverage.cjs src/lib/reviewer-coverage-receipt.test.ts
rollbackPlan: Revert this slice commit to restore prior reviewer-coverage semantics. Existing manifests remain compatible because expectedHeadSha is opt-in.
acceptanceCriteria: Reviewer manifests with expectedHeadSha pass only when each required artifact reports the same 40-character git SHA.
Reviewer manifests with expectedHeadSha fail closed when an artifact reports a stale head SHA.
Reviewer manifests with expectedHeadSha fail closed when an artifact omits head SHA evidence.
Malformed expectedHeadSha values fail as invalid_manifest instead of silently disabling the guard.
Existing manifests without expectedHeadSha continue to use the existing WROTE or blocked-status contract.
circleciCredentialPolicy: Use ~/.codex/.env only for CircleCI API or job-log triage. This slice has not required CircleCI credentials because live public PR checks were already green.
